revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23986660476|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[ja:茨城県護国神社]] {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = 茨城県護国神社 拝殿.jpg | map_type = Japan | coordinates = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_relief = | map_caption = | location = | festival = | type = ''[[Gokoku shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| name = Ibaraki Prefecture Gokoku Shrine | image = [[File:茨城県護国神社_拝殿.jpg|250px]]
Worship Hall | location = 1-2-1 Mikawa, Mito City, Ibaraki Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|36|22|28.8|N|140|26|50.7|E|region:JP-08|display=it}} | deity = {{ill|Fallen Soldiers|ja|戦没者|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fallen Soldiers|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 戦死|1=Killed in action|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q210392}} | type = {{ill|Beppyo shrine|en|Beppyo shrine|qid=Q10898274|lt=Beppyo Shrine|1=Beppyo shrine}}, designated by the Home Minister | established = {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era}} 11 (1878) | festivals = April 10, November 10 | map = {{Switcher |{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=120|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|frame-latitude=36.373|frame-longitude=140.459}} |Show Wikimedia maps |{{location map|Japan Ibaraki|float=center|width=200|label={{nowrap|Ibaraki Prefecture Gokoku Shrine}}|position=left}} |Show map of Ibaraki }} }} '''Ibaraki Prefecture Gokoku Shrine''' (茨城県護国神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located on {{ill|Sakurayama Hill|ja|桜山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurayama Hill|1=Sakurayama Hill|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q119380045}} in {{ill|Kairaku-en|en|Kairaku-en|qid=Q71952|lt=Kairakuen|1=Kairaku-en}}, Mito City, Ibaraki Prefecture. It enshrines war dead from Ibaraki Prefecture. Its crest is a circular sakura motif.. It is a [[Beppyo Shrine]], or a shrine that is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it.{{Cite web |title=別表神社とは？御朱印めぐりに参考になる「別表神社一覧」とマップ {{!}} 開運戦隊ゴシュインジャー |url=https://jinja-gosyuin.com/beppyou/ |access-date=2023-04-02 |website=jinja-gosyuin.com}} It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of {{ill|Yasukuni Shrine|en|Yasukuni Shrine|qid=Q242803|lt=Yasukuni Shrine|1=Yasukuni Shrine}}. They were renamed from {{ill|Shōkonsha|en|Shōkonsha|qid=Q11497810|lt=Shokonsha|1=Shōkonsha}} in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |issn=0021-969X}} [[File:茨城県護国神社_二の鳥居.jpg|200px|thumb|right|Second Torii Gate]] == History == The shrine traces its origins to 1878 (Meiji 11), when approximately 1,800 retainers of the Mito domain, who perished during the Bakumatsu period (starting in 1853), were enshrined in a {{ill|Chinreisha|ja|鎮霊社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinreisha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 靖国神社|1=Yasukuni Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q242803}} located within the grounds of {{ill|Tokiwa shrine|en|Tokiwa shrine|qid=Q3530470|lt=Tokiwa Shrine|1=Tokiwa shrine}}. Over time, additional war dead from Ibaraki Prefecture were enshrined. In 1939, the shrine was renamed {{ill|Chinreisha Gokoku Shrine|ja|鎮霊社護国神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinreisha Gokoku Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135579164|1=Chinreisha Gokoku Shrine}}, and by 1941 it became an independent shrine officially designated by the Home Minister as Ibaraki Prefecture Gokoku Shrine. That same year, it was relocated to its current site on Sakurayama Hill within Kairakuen. After World War II, the shrine was temporarily renamed "Sakurayama Shrine" in 1945 to distance itself from militaristic associations. Following the restoration of Japanese sovereignty in 1954, the original name was reinstated. Currently, the shrine honors 63,494 spirits, including soldiers and civilians affiliated with Ibaraki Prefecture. == Shrine Grounds == [[File:茨城県護国神社_鎮魂碑.jpg|200x200px|thumb|right|Repose Monument]] * '''Sakura no Miya''' ** deity: {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}} ***Located along the main pathway, Sakura no Miya is a small shrine dedicated to the goddess Konohanasakuya-hime, positioned in front of a cherry tree. Numerous memorial monuments are also erected within the shrine grounds. == festivals == The shrine holds two main festivals: * Spring festival: April 10 * Autumn festival: November 10 == Access == * '''Train''': ** {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|qid=Q499071|lt=JR East|1=East Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Jōban Line|en|Jōban Line|qid=Q1369263|lt=Jōban Line|1=Jōban Line}} or {{ill|Kashima Rinkai Railway|en|Kashima Rinkai Railway|qid=Q5370740|lt=Kashima Rinkai Railway|1=Kashima Rinkai Railway}}: {{ill|Mito Station|en|Mito Station|qid=Q11242501|lt=Mito Station|1=Mito Station}} *** Take a {{ill|Ibaraki Kotsu|ja|茨城交通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ibaraki Kotsu|1=Ibaraki Kōtsū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11617285}} bus towards Mikawa and alight at "Sakurayama Stop" (5-minute walk). ** Temporary {{ill|Kairakuen Station|en|Kairakuen Station|qid=Q6348234|lt=Kairakuen Station|1=Kairakuen Station}} during peak seasons. * '''Parking''': Available on site. == See Also == * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * {{ill|Martyrs' shrines (China)|en|Martyrs' shrines (China)|qid=Q6800982|lt=Martyr's Shrines|1=Martyrs' shrines (China)}} * {{ill|Martial temple|en|Martial temple|qid=Q11124210|lt=Martial temple|1=Martial temple}} and {{ill|Wen Wu temple|en|Wen Wu temple|qid=Q7231588|lt=Wen Wu temple|1=Wen Wu temple}} * {{ill|National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine|en|National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine|qid=Q5956488|lt=National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine|1=National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine}} * {{ill|Eternal Spring Shrine|en|Eternal Spring Shrine|qid=Q4501089|lt=Eternal Spring Shrine|1=Eternal Spring Shrine}} * {{ill|Chinese Cultural Renaissance|en|Chinese Cultural Renaissance|qid=Q1073969|lt=Chinese Cultural Renaissance|1=Chinese Cultural Renaissance}} * [[Ancestral shrine]] * {{ill|Gallant Garden|en|Gallant Garden|qid=Q5518867|lt=Gallant Garden|1=Gallant Garden}} * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * {{ill|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|en|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|qid=Q1541043|lt=Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|1=Tomb of the Unknown Soldier}} * {{ill|Arlington National Cemetery|en|Arlington National Cemetery|qid=Q216344|lt=Arlington National Cemetery|1=Arlington National Cemetery}} * {{ill|Valhalla|en|Valhalla|qid=Q177860|lt=Valhalla|1=Valhalla}} (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * {{ill|Walhalla (memorial)|en|Walhalla (memorial)|qid=Q321240|lt=Walhalla Shrine|1=Walhalla (memorial)}} (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * {{ill|Eternal Spring Shrine|en|Eternal Spring Shrine|qid=Q4501089|lt=Eternal Spring Shrine|1=Eternal Spring Shrine}} * {{ill|The common end of myriad good deeds|en|The common end of myriad good deeds|qid=Q9609095|lt=The common end of myriad good deeds|1=The common end of myriad good deeds}} * {{ill|Greek hero cult|en|Greek hero cult|qid=Q1878906|lt=Greek hero cult|1=Greek hero cult}} == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [https://ibaraki-gokokujinja.jp/ Official Website] * [https://twitter.com/559sakura Official Twitter] }} {{shinto-stub}} {{Gokoku Shrines}} {{everybodywiki link|Ibaraki Gokoku Shrine}} [[ja:茨城県護国神社]] {{Commons category|Ibarakiken-Gokoku-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11617516}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Mito, Ibaraki]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:History of Mito, Ibaraki]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
